page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 256 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a top-tier memory subsystem, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and cryptographic execution via PassMark CPU encryption both rank in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth ranging from 64.95 GB/sec for uncached writes to 2198.01 GB/sec for cached reads, and static web serving throughput fall within the average tier. For language model tasks, the instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small and medium models, but exhibits weak performance in prompt processing for large 70B models, which ranks in the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration. The absence of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, representing a potential latency tradeoff. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, image processing, and medium-scale language model text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. The hardware configuration delivers 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU, with no local storage or GPU accelerators included. Performance benchmarks show a top-tier PassMark memory score in the top 10% and strong single-core CPU and encryption performance in the top 25%. However, multi-core processing and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, and large-model prompt processing is weak. Lacking local storage, the instance relies entirely on network-attached storage, presenting a tradeoff for I/O-heavy tasks. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, image processing, and moderate-scale language model text generation.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
